## 2024-03-24 - Initialized
## 2024-03-24 - Prevented CSV Injection
**Vulnerability:** CSV exports generated by Next.js API routes concatenated user input (e.g., name, email, department) directly into CSV lines without sanitization. If a user had an input starting with `=`, `+`, `-`, or `@`, it could execute arbitrary code when the CSV file was opened in Excel or other spreadsheet software (CSV/Formula Injection).
**Learning:** We need to explicitly sanitize any user-controlled inputs before joining them into CSV files exported to Excel.
**Prevention:** Created `lib/csv.ts` with an `escapeCsvFormulas` utility that prefixes these formula triggers with a single quote (`'`), and applied it to all exported CSV fields.
